title: sidalcea malviflora subsp. californica
intro: Sidalcea malviflora subsp. californica, also known as California checkerblooms or pink checkerbloom, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mallow family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, providing relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Sidalcea malviflora subsp. californica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
